title: Alien Romulus Box Office Numbers: Total, Worldwide, Domestic, Opening & Records
text: The 2024 sci-fi horror film "Alien: Romulus" has proven to be a significant commercial success for Disney and 20th Century Studios. Director Fede Álvarez's reboot, released August 16, 2024, generated "$315.3 million worldwide" with a modest "$80 million budget" before marketing costs. The film's opening weekend demonstrated strong audience interest, earning "$41.5 million domestically" and "$66.7 million internationally, for a combined "$108.2 million opening." This represented the second-largest opening in the franchise's history, exceeded only by 2012's "Prometheus". Internationally, the performance was particularly robust, with China contributing "$93.6 million" as the leading territory. The movie surpassed industry expectations that had projected much lower opening figures, ultimately becoming the number-one film that weekend. "Alien: Romulus" now ranks as the second highest-grossing entry in the franchise overall, trailing only "Prometheus". The box office success positions the franchise favorably for future developments, with an upcoming television series "Alien: Earth" already in development alongside potential sequel discussions.
